_meta.title: check_point_indicator_library
_meta.description: Library of customer-facing text for check_point indicators. 14 per-point RTK indicators, 3 blocks. Q-CP locks v2.1: global gate retained, fix_type per-point gate (Q-CP-2), aggregation formula locked (Q-CP-3). Two per-point gates: fix_type and antenna_height (both zero the individual point; aggregation dilutes across fleet).
_meta.text_authorship_status: CLAUDE-DRAFTED PLACEHOLDER. Structure locked, text marked placeholder pending domain refinement.

schema_notes.gate_scope: per_point_only (affects individual point; aggregation dilutes) / none.
schema_notes.per_point_gate_action: When gate fires on a single point, that point is zeroed; other points continue to score and contribute to aggregation.

L3I_CP_001.name: Position sigma
L3I_CP_001.verified_statement: RTK receiver reported position uncertainty (sigma) is within acceptable range.

bands.label: Position sigma 5-15cm (moderate RTK convergence)
bands.impact: RTK locked but with visible uncertainty. Output position has larger error envelope. Acceptable for most survey classes but check whether specification is met.
actions: Verify final deliverable accuracy against project specification
Check whether baseline length or multipath affected convergence
Document sigma value in metadata for downstream reference

bands.label: Position sigma >15cm or no fix (degraded RTK)
bands.impact: RTK either did not converge fully or reported large uncertainty. Position accuracy is compromised; deliverable may not meet specification.
actions: Inspect RTK log for fix-type history and base distance
Check whether multipath or obstructions degraded signal
Consider whether re-occupying under clearer conditions is practical

L3I_CP_001.derivation: Sigma is the anchor indicator for RTK quality. ≤5cm is industry 'good RTK', 5-15cm is 'acceptable with notes', >15cm is 'degraded'.

L3I_CP_002.name: Fix type at capture
L3I_CP_002.verified_statement: RTK receiver had a FIXED integer ambiguity at the measurement epoch (not FLOAT or AUTONOMOUS).

bands.label: FLOAT or AUTONOMOUS (per-point gate fires — Q-CP-2)
bands.impact: RTK did not achieve fixed integer ambiguity. Position is FLOAT (meter-level uncertainty) or AUTONOMOUS (GPS-only, 3-5m uncertainty). This point's score is zeroed per-point aggregation. Other points continue to score.
actions: Inspect RTK log for why fix was not achieved
Check base station corrections availability at the time
If repeated, consider occupying this location longer or from different setup

L3I_CP_002.derivation: Q-CP-2: FLOAT/AUTONOMOUS promoted to per-point gate. Affects individual point; aggregation dilutes across fleet. Not a chain-level hard gate.

L3I_CP_003.name: Correction age
L3I_CP_003.verified_statement: Base station corrections at measurement epoch were recent (not stale).

bands.label: Corrections >30s old (hygiene signal)
bands.impact: RTK used aged corrections. Slight degradation in convergence quality; usually still acceptable but less optimal.
actions: Check whether base station or NTRIP link was temporarily unstable
Inspect whether using a farther base would have better correction freshness

L3I_CP_003.derivation: Correction age >30s means increased atmospheric modeling error in base computation.

L3I_CP_004.name: Log integrity
L3I_CP_004.verified_statement: RTK log file was downloaded cleanly and has valid checksum/signature.

bands.label: Log download incomplete or checksum failed (hygiene signal)
bands.impact: Log file may be corrupted or truncated. Data is still usable but requires inspection for gap or corruption.
actions: Attempt to download log again from device
Inspect log file directly for obvious gaps or truncation
Verify device storage and connectivity for next occupation

L3I_CP_004.derivation: Hygiene signal. Log integrity check catches storage failures early.

L3I_CP_005.name: Antenna height documented
L3I_CP_005.verified_statement: Antenna height measurement was recorded and is traceable.

bands.label: Antenna height not documented (per-point gate)
bands.impact: Height is unknown. Phase center offset cannot be corrected; vertical accuracy is compromised. This point's score is zeroed per aggregation.
actions: Inspect field photo or notes for height evidence
If unavailable, mark this point as un-usable for final coordinates
For next occupation, enforce height measurement protocol

L3I_CP_005.derivation: Q-CP-4: no device-type special handling. All devices must have documented height (unlike GCP where factory-known devices auto-pass).

L3I_CP_006.name: Pole stability
L3I_CP_006.verified_statement: Antenna pole/rod was stable throughout measurement (no movement, no tilt).

bands.label: Pole stable, antenna plumb, no observed movement
Pole had some movement or slight tilt
bands.impact: Antenna may have shifted during measurement. Position reported is averaged over an unstable baseline; accuracy may be degraded.
actions: Inspect field notes/photos for movement evidence
Check whether RTK fix remained constant despite movement
Consider whether re-occupying with more stable setup is practical

bands.label: Pole unstable or tilted significantly
bands.impact: Antenna position changed during measurement. Reported position is unreliable; deliverable may not meet specification.
actions: Mark point as requiring re-occupation with stable setup
Review field protocol for pole stability checks

L3I_CP_006.derivation: Movement during occupation violates RTK assumption (static receiver).

L3I_CP_007.name: Baseline length
L3I_CP_007.verified_statement: Distance to base station or CORS reference is reasonable for RTK convergence.

bands.label: Baseline ≤50 km to base/CORS (standard RTK range)
Baseline >50 km (extended range, slower convergence)
bands.impact: RTK required longer convergence time. Fix quality may be weaker; uncertainty may be larger than nominal.
actions: Check whether a closer base/CORS was available
Verify convergence time matches distance expectation
Inspect final sigma for degradation

L3I_CP_007.derivation: RTK atmospheric modeling errors grow with baseline. >50 km requires extended convergence.

L3I_CP_008.name: NTRIP mountpoint
L3I_CP_008.verified_statement: NTRIP correction stream was correctly matched to location and receiver type.

bands.label: Correct NTRIP mountpoint for location and receiver
NTRIP mountpoint mismatch (wrong region, wrong format)
bands.impact: Corrections may not apply well to location. RTK convergence is suboptimal; final accuracy may not meet specification.
actions: Verify NTRIP stream coverage for the location
Check whether receiver is compatible with correction format
Use correct mountpoint for next occupation

L3I_CP_009.name: Antenna type consistency
L3I_CP_009.verified_statement: Antenna type matches documented type in receiver configuration.

bands.label: Antenna type mismatch
bands.impact: Phase center offset model may be incorrect. Vertical accuracy especially is compromised.
actions: Verify actual antenna installed vs receiver configuration
Update receiver configuration to match actual antenna
If old data, apply post-correction with correct antenna model

L3I_CP_009.derivation: Type mismatch causes incorrect phase center modeling.

L3I_CP_010.name: Device ID provenance
L3I_CP_010.verified_statement: Device serial number in log matches expected device (traceability).

bands.label: Device ID traceable and matches expected unit
Device ID mismatch or not traceable (hygiene)
bands.impact: Audit trail unclear. Cannot verify which physical device was used.
actions: Inspect field notes or device label to verify serial number
For next occupation, confirm device serial before and after use

L3I_CP_011.name: PDOP at capture
L3I_CP_011.verified_statement: Satellite geometry at measurement epoch was favorable (PDOP within RTK range).

bands.label: PDOP 3-6 (acceptable but not ideal)
bands.impact: Satellite geometry was suboptimal. Position uncertainty elevated but usually acceptable.
actions: Check PDOP forecast for future flights
Inspect final sigma for degradation related to PDOP

bands.label: PDOP >6 (poor geometry)
bands.impact: Satellite geometry was poor. RTK output reliability is questionable; final accuracy may not meet specification.
actions: Check whether conditions were forecast; reschedule if possible
Inspect final sigma against tolerance

L3I_CP_011.derivation: PDOP <3 is RTK baseline. >6 indicates poor sky view or unfavorable geometry.

L3I_CP_012.name: Fix hold duration
L3I_CP_012.verified_statement: RTK maintained fixed integer solution continuously through measurement window.

bands.label: Fixed RTK throughout, no cycle slips
Cycle slips or brief float detected
bands.impact: RTK lost fix momentarily. Position average may be slightly degraded; accuracy envelope larger than nominal.
actions: Inspect RTK log for cause of slip (multipath, signal loss, etc.)
Check environment for temporary obstruction or interference
Re-occupy if accuracy-critical

L3I_CP_012.derivation: Cycle slips indicate RTK stability issue. Continuous fix is baseline expectation.

L3I_CP_013.name: Sky obstruction
L3I_CP_013.verified_statement: Clear sky view above antenna — no significant obstruction (buildings, trees, hills).

bands.label: Obstructed sky view (<30° elevation minimum)
bands.impact: Low satellite count. RTK convergence slower; final uncertainty larger. RTK may not achieve fixed solution.
actions: Relocate antenna away from obstructions if possible
Allow longer convergence time at obstructed location
Consider whether alternate location with clearer sky is practical

L3I_CP_013.derivation: RTK requires >4-5 satellites consistently. Obstruction reduces count and quality.

L3I_CP_014.name: Ionospheric risk
L3I_CP_014.verified_statement: Ionospheric conditions during measurement were within historical normal range.

bands.label: Kp 5-7 (active ionosphere, higher variance)
bands.impact: Ionospheric modeling error elevated. RTK convergence may be slower; final uncertainty slightly larger.
actions: Check NOAA SWPC Kp forecast for future surveys; avoid high Kp if possible
Allow longer convergence time during active ionosphere

bands.label: Kp >7 (geomagnetic storm, significant ionospheric disturbance)
bands.impact: Ionospheric disturbance was severe. RTK performance degraded; final accuracy likely compromised.
actions: Reschedule critical observations to quieter periods if possible
Inspect final accuracy against specification

L3I_CP_014.derivation: NOAA SWPC Kp index from external API. High ionospheric disturbance adds modeling error to RTK.
